title: Session display Phase 3 — Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 — Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) — uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan → comment.

title: Terminal: working-status line renders stacked/duplicated (rare) — distinct from #85 garble
body:
## Requirement

The Claude working-status line occasionally renders **stacked/duplicated** — several
successive status frames sit on top of each other (e.g. three `Hashing…` lines with
*different* content: `22 24`, `16s ↓401`, `11s ↓138 · thought for 5s`) instead of the
single line overwriting in place. A resize heals it. **Occurs rarely.** Seen on build
`main @ 7e473e7` (which already carries the DPR-refit fix), WebGL off (DOM renderer).

This is **distinct from #85** (that was the horizontal char-drift *garble* = xterm.js#6015,
addressed by the DPR-refit). This is **vertical duplication** — old status rows not cleared
when the TUI rewrites the status line — a different mechanism.

**Candidate:** xterm.js#5801 (OPEN) — "ED2 (`\x1b[2J`) inside a DEC 2026 synchronized update
block resets viewportY, causing scroll yank during streaming redraws." Matches stacked status
frames during sync redraws. To be confirmed.

**Expected:** a single, in-place-updating status line; no stacking.

## Diagnosis (in progress)

Temporary probe added on a debug branch (never merged): **F5** = pure `terminal.refresh(0, rows-1)`
(render repaint), **F8** = `safeFit` (geometry re-fit) on the focused session. Press each on the
stacked ghost:
- F5 clears it → dirty-row render-ghost → a refresh-heal fixes it.
- F5 fails, F8/resize clears it → geometry/re-fit class.
- Neither → buffer/sync-viewport (xterm.js#5801) → upstream / deeper.

Keep open until the mechanism is identified.

title: Onboarding: first-time-launch welcome screen
body:
Show a welcome screen on the very first launch to orient a new user, then never again unless reset.

## Requirement

- On first launch (fresh install — no global settings yet), show a welcome overlay: brief intro to the app + primary actions.
- **Actions**: "Import settings" (ties into settings import — migrate from another machine) and "Open settings" (first-time configuration), plus a Dismiss/close.
- Dismissing persists a flag so it never shows again (store `welcomeDismissed` in the global settings blob via `merge-setting`, so it rides existing persistence + export/import).
- Style matches existing dialogs (`new-session-overlay` pattern). UI text English.

Depends on the settings export/import feature for the "Import settings" action.

Plan/design in comments.

title: Claude: resuming a session that a live process owns dies with a raw CLI error
body:
## Problem

Switchboard offers **Resume** on every Claude session, including one that a **live process currently owns** — a background agent (`claude --bg`), or an interactive session running in a terminal outside Switchboard. Claude refuses to open such a session twice, so the spawn dies immediately:

```
Session <id> is currently running as a background agent (bg). Use `claude agents` to find and
attach to it, or add --fork-session to branch off a copy.
── session exited (code 1) ──
```

The user clicked an action the app was never able to perform. The spawn path already refuses a launch it knows will fail — a disabled backend, a missing binary, a fork of an id the backend does not know yet (main.js, the guards before `buildLaunch`) — this case is simply not among them.

Two rows in the sidebar can also carry the **same name** (Claude writes the same auto `ai-title` into both transcripts when a background agent and an interactive session work on the same topic), and nothing distinguishes them. It reads as a duplicate entry; it is two sessions, one of which is live and unresumable.

## Requirement

- Do not spawn a resume that cannot succeed. Refuse it before the PTY exists, with a sentence that says why and what to do instead — the way the other pre-spawn guards do.
- Offer the alternative Claude itself names: **fork a copy** (`--resume <id> --fork-session`), which Switchboard already supports.
- Make a session that is currently running elsewhere recognisable in the sidebar, so two identically named rows can be told apart.
- Ownership must be asked of the **backend**, not hardcoded: a backend that cannot answer the question keeps today's behaviour.

## Notes

- Claude answers it directly: `claude agents --json` prints the live sessions (documented in its `--help` as "for scripting; does not require a TTY") — one entry per session with `sessionId`, `kind` (`background` | `interactive`), `pid`, `status`, `name`, `cwd`.
- It costs **~1.5 s** per call, so it cannot go on the scan path uncached.
- `~/.claude/jobs/<short-id>/state.json` records the same session (`template: "bg"`), but its `state` reads `done` while the daemon still holds the session — the job state is NOT a liveness signal. The agent list is.

title: Command palette (Ctrl/Cmd+K) to jump to sessions/projects and run actions
body:
## Requirement

A keyboard-driven command palette, opened with Ctrl/Cmd+K from anywhere in the app, that fuzzy-matches across sessions, projects, and app-level actions and runs the selected entry.

## Behaviour

- Ctrl/Cmd+K toggles a modal overlay; Esc closes it; the rest of the UI is inert while open.
- Typing fuzzy-filters a single ranked list combining:
  - **Sessions** — matched on display name / AI title / summary, most-recent-first as the default (empty query) view.
  - **Projects** — matched on display name.
  - **Actions** — a registry of app commands: toggle grid overview, open global settings, add project, collapse/expand all sections, re-sort sessions, focus next attention.
- Arrow keys move the selection, Enter runs it (open the session/project, or invoke the action), Esc closes.
- Selecting a session opens it via the existing open-session path; selecting a project reveals/focuses it in the sidebar.

## Constraints (this codebase)

- Renderer is vanilla JS, no framework, morphdom. New module lives under \`src/renderer/\`.
- Matching/ranking must be a **pure, DOM-free module** with unit tests under \`test/\`; the overlay/keyboard-loop file only wires DOM to it.
- Reuse the existing overlay pattern from \`src/renderer/terminal/variable-palette.js\` (keyboard-driven picker) rather than inventing new modal chrome.
- A new control inherits no styling — reuse existing classes, no bare elements.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+K opens/closes the palette from any focus state.
- [ ] Query filters sessions, projects and actions in one ranked list; empty query shows recent sessions.
- [ ] Enter runs the highlighted entry (open session/project or run action); Esc closes.
- [ ] Ranking/matching logic is a pure module covered by \`node --test\`.
- [ ] The overlay reuses existing styling; no unstyled controls.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ — open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** — needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** — when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` → `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents — meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss — pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` → placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Make the terminal LRU cap configurable
body:
The renderer keeps at most 12 live xterm instances (`TERMINAL_LRU_CAP` in `public/terminal-manager.js`); the least-recently-shown closed session beyond the cap is destroyed (soft cap: running and active sessions are never evicted).

Since WebGL is the default renderer, the cap also serves as the GL-context budget (Chromium allows ~16 per process; a lost context falls back to the DOM renderer).

Expose the cap as a setting for users with many parallel sessions and enough RAM. Constraints: keep 12 as the default; values above ~14 should hint that terminals beyond the GL budget silently fall back to the DOM renderer.

Deferred from #81 (was rejected there as "defer until the cap is hit").

title: Evaluate: local Whisper voice input
body: Evaluate porting ivandobsky `feat/local-whisper-voice` — local Whisper voice control/input. Source: ivandobskygithub/switchboard @ feat/local-whisper-voice.

title: Evaluate: brianstanley cursor-ui-refresh
body: Evaluate brianstanley `cursor-ui-refresh` — UI refresh. Source: brianstanley/switchboard @ cursor-ui-refresh. We already ported brianstanley's Variables + preview panel; check what's new/portable.

title: Optional second tray icon showing Claude usage (live percentage)
body:
Add an optional **second system-tray icon** that shows current Claude usage, enabled via Settings (off by default).

## Requirement
- A live-rendered tray icon showing the current usage percentage, styled like a usage monitor: a **colored progress ring** or a **badge** with the number baked into the icon, color-coded by the existing warn/critical thresholds (green → amber → red).
- **Tooltip** with the details we already show in the status bar (5h / 7d, extra usage, reset time).
- Small **context menu**: show/hide the window, open Stats, refresh, quit.
- **Cross-platform**: macOS, Windows, Linux.
- **Reuse the usage data the app already has** — the same values the status bar renders. **No separate polling loop / no extra network fetch** for the tray; it updates whenever the existing status-bar usage updates.
- Enabled + styled via a **Settings** toggle (icon style ring/badge). When off, the second tray is not created; toggling off destroys it.

Reference behaviour: a small pystray + Pillow usage-monitor tray that draws a ring/badge with the percentage and threshold colors.

title: Terminal: evaluate VSCode-style shell integration (OSC 633) for plain terminals
body:
Idea from the VSCode draw-path comparison (#87/#114 context). VSCode's shell integration injects a shell profile hook that emits OSC 633 sequences (command start / command end / exit code / cwd), enabling command tracking, "run recent command", per-command scrollback navigation, and precise prompt detection.

**Requirement:** evaluate whether a slim variant is worth it for our **plain terminals** (not Claude sessions — the CLI is a fullscreen TUI where command tracking is meaningless): e.g. exit-code badges, jump-to-previous-command in scrollback, or more reliable "shell is idle" detection. Likely low value for this app's core use case; document a decision either way. Large feature — only pursue with a concrete user-facing benefit.

title: i18n: renderer localisation support layer (language-neutral, EN master)
body:
Add a language-neutral localisation **support layer** to the renderer, so any user-facing UI string can be translated. This issue is about the **infrastructure**, not the translations themselves — English stays the master/source of truth; concrete languages are added on top afterwards.

## Requirement (the support layer)

- A global `t('area.key', { placeholders })` available to every renderer script — no build step, no module system (matches our vanilla `<script>` setup).
- **English is the master.** Missing keys fall back to English, then to the key itself — the UI is never blank.
- Static HTML localised via `data-i18n` / `data-i18n-title` / `data-i18n-placeholder` / `data-i18n-aria` attributes, filled on load.
- Active language is a global setting (Settings UI). Changing it persists and re-renders every view in the new language.
- Extract the existing English UI strings (sidebar tabs/chrome, session filters, settings labels/dialogs/tooltips) into the dictionary, keyed. No behaviour change while only EN exists.
- Language-neutral by design: adding a language = adding one dictionary, no code changes.

## Rollout (separate follow-up issues, not this one)

Language order once the layer lands: **EN (master) → DE → FR**. Each added language is its own translation task on top of this support layer.

## Work organisation

Large, cross-cutting change (touches nearly every renderer file that emits text) → **own feature branch**.

Reference implementation for the layer: kreaddis `public/i18n.js` (~488 lines) — adapt the mechanism, ignore its specific fr strings. Upstream commit `6e4d127` (fr apostrophe fix) is only relevant once translations exist.

Plan/design + string-extraction strategy go in comments.

title: perf: run the live-session identity match (matchLiveSession) in the index worker
body:
## Problem

#209 measured the on-main cost of `matchLiveSession` (the birth-time identity match that pairs a freshly
spawned Axis-B session with the transcript the CLI just named) and cut it with a filename-derived birth
pre-filter — 82% off a 5000-transcript store. What is left is the part a filter cannot remove: the
**`discoverSessions()` readdir walk itself**, plus the stat of every transcript born inside the skew margin.

Measured after the #209 fix, per watcher flush while a session is still unpaired, on the main thread:

| transcripts | matchLiveSession |
|---|---|
| 500 | ~17 ms |
| 2000 | ~27 ms |
| 5000 | ~44 ms |

Bounded (it stops the moment the session pairs, normally after 1–2 flushes) and an order below the stall
#199 removed — but it is the last store walk the main thread still does, and it grows with the store.

## Requirement

Run the identity match off the main thread, through the persistent index worker: main posts the match
request (backendId + cwd + sinceMs + the claimed set), the worker runs `matchLiveSession` (it is
Electron-free and the worker already resolves backends by id), main claims the reply.

Known costs to design for, none of them blockers, all of them the reason this is filed rather than done:

- **`claimLiveRecord` becomes async**, and `updateBackendLiveStates` is today a synchronous loop that
  re-keys `activeSessions` mid-iteration. The re-key would move to a later tick — the snapshot-to-avoid-
  mutation logic has to be rethought.
- **New race surface**, the same shape #208 needed guards for: a session that exits while its match is in
  flight; two flushes posting two match requests for one session (needs per-session coalescing); a stale
  reply arriving after another claimed the record.
- **Latency**: the id binding and the first busy/idle push wait a worker round-trip, so a brand-new
  non-Claude session shows its temp id a beat longer. Claude's temp→real transition already tolerates this.
- **The walk is relocated, not removed** — the worker then pays it, and a match competing with the reconcile
  and file lanes needs a sensible place in the priority queue.

## Acceptance

- No store walk or transcript read runs on the main thread in the backend-store watcher flush.
- A freshly launched Codex/Pi/agy session still binds to its real session id and shows live busy/idle,
  without a restart; a resumed session still claims its record via `liveRefFor`.
- `npm test` green.

Follow-up to #209 (design record + the measured numbers: `docs/specs/11-performance.md`).

title: Session lineage — remaining work (per-backend provenance + display polish)
body:
Central place to continue the **session lineage / provenance** feature (#193 + #223). What shipped, and what is left.

## Shipped (on `main`)

Lineage is **backend-neutral**: one sink (`src/index/index-writes.js applyIndexResults`) stamps `lineageParentId`/`lineageKind` by calling `backends.get(backendId).resolveLineage(row)` — the core never reads a backend's format or branches on an id. Each descriptor declares the hook (Claude → fork, Hermes → parent, Codex/Pi/agy → `null` honest gaps). Rendering (Model A, `src/renderer/shell/sidebar-lineage.js`): the live/newest session is the head, idle ancestors fold under a `▶ N earlier` caret (the subagent-nesting affordance), each ancestor a **full session row** with all its actions; a live ancestor stays its own row. Spec 13 + `docs/backend-formats.md` document it. Verified by an adversarial verifier (twice) + live in the app.

**The multi-session `/clear` re-key is solved (#223, closed).** Switchboard writes a per-terminal settings file and passes it as `--settings`; its single `SessionEnd`/`clear` hook makes the CLI report which terminal cleared which session. That claim resolves the parent before the old single-session rule, so a `/clear` re-keys correctly even with several sessions live in one folder. Verified live in the installed app with two sessions in one project: the cleared row moves to the new id, the neighbour is untouched, no orphan row. `supportsLiveRebinding` + `buildLiveBinding`/`releaseLiveBinding` are descriptor hooks, so Codex' `/new` can implement the same shape. Commits `1202221`, `c33dff6`.

Earlier commits: `fab1efc`, `5066e2b` (#223 resolver), `52fed15`, `9c1ff59` (#193 data + first render), `85b8d4d`, `83e4450` (neutral refactor + profile fix), `4dc7cae`, `98d7aff`, `b974db6`, `8341c86` (rendering: caret → drop caption → full rows → docs). CLAUDE.md now records the neutral-descriptor rule for any cross-backend feature.

## Open — the actual continuation work

- [ ] **Wire lineage for the backends that currently return `null`** — only when the signal is verified against the real format (see the CLAUDE.md rule): Codex (`/clear` starts a new rollout with no back-ref; `compacted` is a state, not a parent), Pi (`--fork` exists but the header records no parent), agy (`parent_references` is an unschema'd protobuf blob — reverse-engineer against a real forked trajectory). Each is a descriptor + reader edit, no core change. **Live rebinding is the same shape**: Codex' `/new` could implement `buildLiveBinding`/`releaseLiveBinding` if its CLI offers an equivalent hook.
- [ ] **Hard/soft visual marker** — the hard (fork/parent) vs soft (`/clear` guess) distinction lives only in the data (`lineageKind`) now; there is no visual cue. Cheap follow-up if wanted: a dimmed/italic ancestor row (or a subtle badge) for `lineageKind === 'clear'`. Deliberately deferred as "not necessary".
- [ ] **Cosmetic**: ~~an expanded thread collapses on the next sidebar re-render (morphdom re-applies `display:none`)~~ — **done in `49e0e3e`**; a very long `/clear` chain is not capped in the expander; a live ancestor also appears inside a descendant's expander (consistent with Model A's shared-ancestor stance). Also: a re-keyed session takes its title from the child transcript's first line, which for a `/clear` is the command text itself (`/clear clear …`).
- [ ] **Same-session compaction** (Claude `logicalParentUuid`, Codex `compacted`) is out of scope — not a cross-session parent, so not a lineage row. Noted so it is not re-litigated.

Related: #193 (the display), #223 (the re-key, closed), #242 (two terminals clearing at the same moment — deliberate bail).

- [ ] **Thread expansion is DOM state only, so it dies whenever the node is rebuilt.** `49e0e3e` carries it
  across a morphdom pass, but not across anything that changes the head row's DOM id: a `/clear` re-key
  (#223) mints a new `si-<id>` and the thread folds — on the very event that created the lineage. A search
  does the same, because that path skips the fold and renders the ancestor top-level *and* inside the
  expander. The subagent caret this affordance deliberately copies does not have the problem: it persists
  its expanded set in localStorage and re-applies it at build time. Mirroring that (an `expandedLineage`
  set keyed by head session id) would make the thread survive re-keys, search and restarts alike.
- [ ] **`onBeforeElUpdated` has no test coverage.** Its preservation clauses are an anonymous callback
  inside the render, so the regression fixed in `49e0e3e` was only catchable by driving the app. Pulling
  them into a pure `preserve(fromEl, toEl)` beside the other sidebar-state helpers would make all five
  testable at once.

title: Two terminals clearing the same folder at the same moment stay ambiguous
body:
#223 ties a `/clear` to the terminal it happened in: the CLI's `SessionEnd` hook reports
"terminal *X* ended session *Y* by clear", and that claim picks the right parent even with
several sessions live in one project folder.

One case stays unresolved on purpose. If **two terminals in the same folder clear within the
same claim window**, there are two claims and two new transcripts, and nothing in either
signal says which child belongs to which parent — the CLI reports the *old* id on
`SessionEnd`, never the new one (`SessionStart` does not fire from a `--settings` file, measured
against CLI v2.1.215). So the resolver bails and both sessions fall back to the pre-#223
behaviour: the source keeps its row and tab until it exits, the child appears as its own row.

That bail is the correct conservative answer today — mis-keying a session is worse than not
keying it. This issue records the gap and what would close it:

- A signal that names the **child** id per terminal. If a future CLI fires `SessionStart` from a
  `--settings` file (or `SessionEnd` gains the successor id), the pairing becomes exact and the
  ambiguity disappears entirely.
- Failing that: ordering. Both claims and both transcripts carry timestamps; pairing them in
  order is a heuristic, not a fact, and would need real measurement before it could be trusted
  (the mtime heuristic that preceded #223 was wrong in ~95 % of cases).

Low priority — it needs two people, or one person with two terminals, clearing within seconds
of each other in the same project.

Refs #223, #229

title: Terminal: flow-control watermarks and single/tabs scrollback ~10x above VS Code
body:
The write-flow watermarks and the single/tabs scrollback are about an order of
magnitude larger than VS Code's, which shows up as parse-latency backlog and
renderer memory. VS Code uses a 100k/5k char high/low watermark and a 1000-line
scrollback default; the app uses a 1MB/256KB byte watermark and a 10000-line
scrollback (the grid scrollback is already 1000, matching VS Code).

**Requirement:** bring the single/tabs scrollback and the flow-control
watermarks down toward the VS Code order of magnitude, and expose scrollback as
a setting (update `docs/settings-reference.md`). This is a measured change, not
a guess — capture before/after end-to-end latency on a large `cat` and renderer
RSS, and pick values from the measurement.

title: Prompt queue: stage prompts per session and auto-deliver when ready
body:
## Requirement

Let the user queue one or more prompts for a session and have them delivered automatically once the session is safe to receive typed input.

## Behaviour

- The user can add a prompt to a session's queue while the session is busy or needs attention.
- A queued prompt is delivered **only** when the session status is \`response-ready\` or \`idle\`.
- Never deliver into \`busy\` (would interleave with an in-flight turn), \`needs-attention\` (typed text would pick a permission-prompt menu option instead of reaching the agent), or \`exited\` (no process).
- Items deliver one at a time, in order; the next waits for the session to return to a deliverable status.
- The user can view the pending items for a session and remove/reorder them.

## Constraints (this codebase)

- Renderer is vanilla JS, no framework. UI module under \`src/renderer/\`; a pure queue-store module (create store, enqueue, next-deliverable, remove) under it with unit tests in \`test/\`.
- The queue store must be a plain serializable object (no hidden state) so it can be persisted/inspected.
- Gate delivery on the existing session busy/idle state model (the same signal that drives the status indicators).

## Acceptance

- [ ] A prompt can be queued on a busy / needs-attention session.
- [ ] The queue delivers the next item automatically when status becomes \`response-ready\` or \`idle\`.
- [ ] No delivery ever happens in \`busy\`, \`needs-attention\`, or \`exited\`.
- [ ] Queued items are visible per session and can be removed/reordered.
- [ ] Queue-store logic is a pure module covered by \`node --test\`.

title: Quick actions on session cards (approve/deny/reply) driven by live status
body:
## Requirement

Show contextual quick-action buttons on a session card that let the user respond to the session without focusing its terminal — e.g. Approve, Deny, Reply — based on the session's live status.

## Behaviour

- Quick actions render only when the session has a live PTY and its status warrants them (e.g. a permission prompt awaiting approval).
- Approve / Deny send the runtime's approval key sequence into the session; Reply opens a small input that types the entered text into the session.
- When there is no live PTY, no quick actions are shown.

## Constraints (this codebase)

- Per-runtime key bindings (approval/deny keys) are a **backend descriptor hook** — a capability that varies per backend. No \`switch (backendId)\` in the core, no \`|| 'claude'\` fallback. The keys come from each backend's descriptor so a runtime that binds different keys only touches its own folder.
- Renderer is vanilla JS, no framework; new controls reuse existing button classes (no bare \`<button>\`).
- The action set for a given status must be pure/testable (input: status + hasLivePty + runtime → list of actions).

## Acceptance

- [ ] A session at a permission prompt with a live PTY shows Approve / Deny / Reply.
- [ ] Sessions without a live PTY show no quick actions.
- [ ] Approval/deny keys are read from the backend descriptor, not a core switch or a hardcoded default.
- [ ] Reply types the entered text into the session.
- [ ] The status-to-actions mapping is a pure module covered by \`node --test\`.

title: Sidebar start state: add 'auto' option to collapse stale projects on open
body:
## Requirement

Add an `auto` value to the sidebar project start-state setting (`sidebarCollapseDefault`), which
currently offers `expanded` / `collapsed` / `remember`. In `auto` mode, projects whose newest
session is older than a dedicated age threshold start collapsed on app open, while recent ones
start expanded; a manual collapse/expand always wins (see Behaviour).

Introduce a **dedicated threshold setting** `sidebarCollapseAgeDays` for this — do **not** reuse
`sessionMaxAgeDays`. `sessionMaxAgeDays` governs the per-project session visible/older split (#144),
a different concept; coupling both to one knob would prevent tuning them independently. Default
`sidebarCollapseAgeDays` to the current `sessionMaxAgeDays` value so existing behaviour is preserved.

## Behaviour

- Setting values become: `auto` / `expanded` / `collapsed` / `remember`.
- `auto`: on app open, collapse a project when its most recent session is older than
  `sidebarCollapseAgeDays`; leave the rest expanded. A manual collapse/expand of a project is
  persisted and **always overrides** the age heuristic — same rule the hidden heuristic follows
  today. `auto` must not stomp user toggles on every start.
- The age-based auto-collapse heuristic already exists as a **hidden** fallback in the sidebar
  builder, currently gated on `sessionMaxAgeDays` and active for never-toggled projects in
  `remember`. `auto` makes it a selectable, first-class value on its own threshold.
- `remember` becomes **pure last-state**: it no longer applies the age heuristic (never-toggled
  projects start expanded).
- `expanded` / `collapsed` keep their current behaviour (force all).
- **Migration:** existing `remember` users are moved to `auto` on upgrade, so they keep the
  auto-collapse behaviour they effectively have today (heuristic + manual toggles win). Without
  this, `remember` users silently lose auto-collapse and see all old projects expanded — reads like
  a bug.

## Constraints (this codebase)

- Renderer is vanilla JS, no framework; the setting select lives in the global-settings HTML and
  its default in the app settings defaults.
- `sidebarCollapseAgeDays = 0 = unlimited` → `auto` collapses nothing (mirror the existing
  `sessionMaxAgeDays = 0` semantics).
- Labels/descriptions must state the criterion, not just say "Auto": name that recently-active
  projects stay open, and note the `0 = collapses nothing` case in the `auto` description.
- A changed/added setting must be reflected in `docs/settings-reference.md`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] The setting offers auto / expanded / collapsed / remember.
- [ ] `auto` collapses projects whose newest session is older than `sidebarCollapseAgeDays` on open,
      and expands the rest.
- [ ] A manual collapse/expand of a project persists and overrides `auto`.
- [ ] `remember` no longer applies the age heuristic (pure last-state).
- [ ] Existing `remember` users are migrated to `auto` on upgrade.
- [ ] New setting `sidebarCollapseAgeDays` defaults to the current `sessionMaxAgeDays` value.
- [ ] With `sidebarCollapseAgeDays = 0`, `auto` collapses nothing.
- [ ] `docs/settings-reference.md` documents the new value, the new threshold, and defaults.

title: Explore: rendered Markdown editing in the internal editor (approach TBD)
body:
The internal viewer (`ViewerPanel`) edits Markdown as source (CodeMirror) with a read-only rendered preview toggle. There is demand to edit Markdown in a rendered form, but the right approach is not decided yet — this issue is for discussion before any implementation.

**Requirement:** Decide how (and whether) to offer rendered Markdown editing, weighing the trade-offs. Candidate directions:

- **WYSIWYG / rich editor** — a real Markdown editor (ProseMirror / TipTap / Milkdown). Pulls in a framework + bundling, which conflicts with the renderer's "vanilla JS, no framework, no bundler beyond the esbuild CodeMirror bundle" model. Costs: bundle size, build complexity (`build:win`/NSIS), the classic-script global-scope guard suite (`script-order.json`, `renderer-no-undef`, `ALLOWED_BINDINGS`), a second markdown parse/serialize round-trip (fidelity + security surface vs. the current `marked`→`DOMPurify` path, #49), and fork/merge maintenance.
- **Live split view** — keep CodeMirror source editing, add a synchronized rendered preview alongside (source left, rendered right). Stays entirely in-stack, no framework. Cheaper, but not true in-place rendered editing.
- **Other** — e.g. inline preview widgets, or leaving it at the source editor.

Related: #279 (default preview vs. code mode). Performance is not the deciding factor here — the trade-off is bundle size, build/guard model, and markdown round-trip fidelity.

title: Changes window: expand large diffs into a standalone CodeMirror window
body:
## Requirement

In the VCS changes window (#277 / #285), the diff currently expands inline under a file row. For a file with a **large** diff that is cramped, offer an **expand** button that opens the file's diff in a **standalone window** with a richer view. The inline diff stays for small changes; the expand button appears only when the diff is large (beyond a line threshold).

## Behaviour

- The inline diff pane gains an **expand / open-in-window** control, shown **only when the diff is large** (e.g. more than ~200 lines).
- Clicking it opens a standalone window rendering that one file's diff full-size — reuse the per-repo window pattern (`src/app/vcs.js`), keyed by cwd + path, destroy-on-close.
- Use **CodeMirror** (already bundled as `codemirror-bundle.js`) for a side-by-side diff with syntax highlighting, instead of the plain colored-line view the inline pane uses.
- Small diffs are unaffected — they stay inline, no button.
